narrative.context: You’re about to present. Your heart races and your mind goes blank. You feel panic building.
narrative.prompt: What is the most effective emotion management strategy right now?

options.label: Ground + slow breathing, then one cue card
options.description: Use a brief breathing cycle, feel your feet on the ground, then focus on your first 2 talking points.

ei.rationale: Down-regulates panic and redirects attention to manageable steps—effective short-term emotion management.

options.label: Distract briefly to reduce arousal
options.description: Look away, sip water, and try to calm down before starting.

ei.rationale: May reduce arousal, but lacks a clear plan to re-engage attention and confidence.

options.label: Push through while criticizing yourself
options.description: Tell yourself you’re failing and force yourself to start immediately.

ei.rationale: Self-criticism can increase threat response; emotion becomes harder to manage.

options.label: Escape the situation
options.description: Leave the room or ask to skip without trying regulation.

ei.rationale: Avoids managing the emotion; reinforces panic over time.
